questions.question: In the context of low-power digital IC design, which technique is primarily used to reduce dynamic power consumption by disabling the clock signal to idle functional units or registers?
options: Clock Gating
Power Gating
Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling (DVFS)
Fine-grained Memory Access
Operand Isolation

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a fundamental design principle of a Reduced Instruction Set Computer (RISC) architecture?
options: Instructions are typically microcoded and of variable length.
Most operations can directly manipulate operands in memory.
A large and complex set of specialized instructions is provided.
It employs a load/store architecture with fixed-length instructions.
It prioritizes maximizing instruction density over pipeline efficiency.

questions.question: In the context of System-on-Chip (SoC) design and verification, what is the primary advantage offered by Transaction-Level Modeling (TLM)?
options: It enables precise cycle-accurate simulation for gate-level netlists.
It simplifies the process of creating physical floorplans and power grids.
It allows for early architectural exploration and fast simulation by abstracting away low-level timing and communication details.
It is primarily used for formal verification of security protocols at the instruction set level.
It helps in determining optimal placement and routing of standard cells.

questions.question: Which of the following represents the primary user-configurable logic element that forms the core programmable fabric of a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)?
options: Application-Specific Integrated Circuits (ASICs)
Microprocessors (CPUs)
Look-Up Tables (LUTs)
Digital Signal Processors (DSPs)
Hardwired logic gates (AND, OR, NOT)

questions.question: In the context of computer architecture and memory hierarchy, what is the primary objective of 'cache prefetching'?
options: To guarantee that all cache lines are kept coherent across multiple CPU cores.
To improve system security by encrypting data before it enters the cache.
To proactively fetch data into the cache that is anticipated to be used soon, thereby reducing future memory access latency.
To implement a least-recently-used (LRU) algorithm for cache line eviction.
To compress data stored in the cache to increase effective capacity.
